acceptance_criteria.text: Backend exposes regeneration start/status endpoints, selects only needs_review, and limits to 10.
proof: Implemented POST /api/services/regenerate-problematic and GET /api/services/regenerate-problematic/<job_id> in src/api/services_api.py.
Selection logic in src/core/service_problem_regeneration.py filters audit needs_review and applies SERVICE_REGENERATION_BATCH_LIMIT = 10.
Production unauthenticated route checks returned 401 for POST and GET, proving routes are registered behind auth.

acceptance_criteria.text: Regeneration runs as a background job and tracks fixed/failed/manual/remaining counts.
proof: _run_service_regeneration_job runs in a daemon thread, calls existing /api/services/optimize, stores optimized_name/optimized_description, and updates job fixed/failed/manual_review/remaining.
UI polls the job status endpoint until completed/rate_limited/failed.

acceptance_criteria.text: Repeated failed services are protected from infinite regeneration cycles.
proof: SERVICE_REGENERATION_ATTEMPTS and MAX_SERVICE_REGENERATION_ATTEMPTS mark repeat candidates as manual_review.
gaps: Attempt memory is process-local by design for this no-migration stage.

acceptance_criteria.text: UI has a problem-regeneration button, limit context, status polling, and refresh after completion.
proof: CardServicesControls shows 'Перегенерировать проблемные' and 'До 10 услуг за запуск'.
useCardServiceController starts the backend job, polls status, and reloads services.

acceptance_criteria.text: Telegram card menu can start problematic regeneration from services audit context.
proof: Telegram callback creates a scoped session for the linked user and calls POST /api/services/regenerate-problematic.

acceptance_criteria.text: Quality history is logged without a new complex DB schema.
proof: Job start, per-service regeneration, and job finish are recorded through record_ai_learning_event with capability services.regenerate_problematic.

known_gaps: The job queue and retry-attempt memory are in-process for this no-migration stage; they reset when app restarts.
Container py_compile with pycache writes is blocked by read-only /app/src, so runtime source compile was verified with Python compile(...).
